projectDescription: A fast, open-source vulnerability scanner for container images, filesystems, and SBOMs that supports dozens of OS and language package ecosystems.
keyFeatures: Scan container images, filesystems, and SBOMs for known vulnerabilities
Supports major OS package ecosystems (Alpine, Debian, Ubuntu, RHEL, Amazon Linux, and more)
Supports language-specific packages (Go, Java, Python, Ruby, JavaScript, Rust, .NET, and more)
Threat prioritization with EPSS, KEV, and risk scoring
OpenVEX and CSAF support for filtering and augmenting scan results
Multiple output formats: table, JSON, CycloneDX, SARIF, and custom Go templates
Configurable ignore rules to suppress known false positives
Offline-capable with a local vulnerability database
Docker, OCI, Podman, and Singularity image format support
publicApiSurface: grype [IMAGE] — scan a container image for vulnerabilities
grype dir:[PATH] — scan a local filesystem directory
grype sbom:[PATH] — scan an SBOM file
grype file:[PATH] — scan a single file
grype registry:[IMAGE] — pull and scan directly from a registry
grype db update — update the vulnerability database
grype db status — show vulnerability database status
grype db search — search the vulnerability database
grype db diff — diff two vulnerability databases
grype db list — list available vulnerability databases
grype explain --id [CVE-ID] — explain a specific vulnerability finding
-f [SEVERITY] / --fail-on — set severity threshold for non-zero exit code
--vex [FILE] — apply a VEX document to filter results
--ignore — configure ignore rules via config file or flags
--scope squashed|all-layers — configure image layer scanning scope
